outlook.headline: AI storage demand is broadening across HDDs and enterprise SSDs
outlook.summary: Demand for AI storage looks set to improve over the next 1-2 quarters, with cloud-led HDD capacity demand and enterprise SSD pull both described as hot by the key suppliers. Seagate and Western Digital see tight supply and customer-driven HAMR/Mozaic ramps, while SanDisk points to enormous enterprise SSD demand as AI architectures scale; Micron is more neutral, so the main watch is whether the strength stays broad rather than HDD-only.

drivers.label: Cloud-driven HDD capacity demand
drivers.detail: AI-driven demand for higher-capacity HDDs is supporting customer investment and exabyte growth.

drivers.label: HAMR / next-gen HDD ramp
drivers.detail: Roadmap progress, qualification, and Mozaic/HAMR shipments are being pulled by customers, indicating durable demand into the next quarters.

drivers.label: Enterprise SSD pull from AI deployments
drivers.detail: Enterprise SSD demand is described as enormous as AI architectures deploy at scale.

drivers.label: Tight supply backdrop
drivers.detail: Tight supply is reinforcing shipment momentum and points to continued demand absorption.

risks: HAMR qualification or ramp timing slips at the HDD suppliers
Enterprise SSD demand normalizes after the current AI deployment wave
Micron’s more neutral read suggests SSD demand breadth is not yet fully confirmed
